This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"revokePauser(address)":{"params":{"pauser":"The revoked address."}},"setSplits((uint256,uint32)[])":{"params":{"receivers":"The list of the account's splits receivers to be set. Must be sorted by the splits receivers' addresses, deduplicated and without 0 weights. Each splits receiver will be getting `weight / TOTAL_SPLITS_WEIGHT` share of the funds collected by the account. If the sum of weights of all receivers is less than `_TOTAL_SPLITS_WEIGHT`, some funds won't be split, but they will be left for the account to collect. It's valid to include the account's own `accountId` in the list of receivers, but funds split to themselves return to their splittable balance and are not collectable. This is usually unwanted, because if splitting is repeated, funds split to themselves will be again split using the current configuration. Splitting 100% to self effectively blocks splitting unless the configuration is updated."}},"setStreams(address,(uint256,uint256)[],int128,(uint256,uint256)[],uint32,uint32,address)":{"params":{"balanceDelta":"The streams balance change to be applied. Positive to add funds to the streams balance, negative to remove them.","currReceivers":"The current streams receivers list. It must be exactly the same as the last list set for the sender with `setStreams`. If this is the first update, pass an empty array.","erc20":"The used ERC-20 token. It must preserve amounts, so if some amount of tokens is transferred to an address, then later the same amount must be transferable from that address. Tokens which rebase the holders' balances, collect taxes on transfers, or impose any restrictions on holding or transferring tokens are not supported. If you use such tokens in the protocol, they can get stuck or lost.","maxEndHint1":"An optional parameter allowing gas optimization, pass `0` to ignore it. The first hint for finding the maximum end time when all streams stop due to funds running out after the balance is updated and the new receivers list is applied. Hints have no effect on the results of calling this function, except potentially saving gas. Hints are Unix timestamps used as the starting points for binary search for the time when funds run out in the range of timestamps from the current block's to `2^32`. Hints lower than the current timestamp are ignored. You can provide zero, one or two hints. The order of hints doesn't matter. Hints are the most effective when one of them is lower than or equal to the last timestamp when funds are still streamed, and the other one is strictly larger than that timestamp,the smaller the difference between such hints, the higher gas savings. The savings are the highest possible when one of the hints is equal to the last timestamp when funds are still streamed, and the other one is larger by 1. It's worth noting that the exact timestamp of the block in which this function is executed may affect correctness of the hints, especially if they're precise. Hints don't provide any benefits when balance is not enough to cover a single second of streaming or is enough to cover all streams until timestamp `2^32`. Even inaccurate hints can be useful, and providing a single hint or two hints that don't enclose the time when funds run out can still save some gas. Providing poor hints that don't reduce the number of binary search steps may cause slightly higher gas usage than not providing any hints.","maxEndHint2":"An optional parameter allowing gas optimization, pass `0` to ignore it. The second hint for finding the maximum end time, see `maxEndHint1` docs for more details.","newReceivers":"The list of the streams receivers of the sender to be set. Must be sorted by the receivers' addresses, deduplicated and without 0 amtPerSecs.","transferTo":"The address to send funds to in case of decreasing balance"},"returns":{"realBalanceDelta":"The actually applied streams balance change."}},"upgradeTo(address)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event."},"upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event."}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"NewAdminProposed(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when a new admin of the contract is proposed. Sets the proposed admin to the zero address. Can only be called by the proposed admin."},"admin()":{"notice":"Returns the address of the current admin."},"allPausers()":{"notice":"Returns all the addresses with the pauser role."},"calcAccountId(address)":{"notice":"Calculates the account ID for an address. Every account ID is a 256-bit integer constructed by concatenating: `driverId (32 bits) | zeros (64 bits) | addr (160 bits)`."},"collect(address,address)":{"notice":"Collects the account's received already split funds and transfers them out of the Drips contract."},"drips()":{"notice":"The Drips address used by this driver."},"driverId()":{"notice":"The driver ID which this driver uses when calling Drips."},"emitAccountMetadata((bytes32,bytes)[])":{"notice":"Emits the account metadata for the message sender. The keys and the values are not standardized by the protocol, it's up to the users to establish and follow conventions to ensure compatibility with the consumers."},"forceCollect(address,address,address,address,bytes)":{"notice":"Force collect - for when funds are in YieldManager instead of Drips vault"},"give(uint256,address,uint128)":{"notice":"Gives funds from the message sender to the receiver. The receiver can split and collect them immediately. Transfers the funds to be given from the message sender's wallet to the Drips contract."},"grantPauser(address)":{"notice":"Grants the pauser role to an address. Callable only by the admin."},"implementation()":{"notice":"Returns the current implementation address."},"isPaused()":{"notice":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"isPauser(address)":{"notice":"Checks if an address is a pauser."},"pause()":{"notice":"Triggers stopped state. Callable only by the admin or a pauser."},"proposeNewAdmin(address)":{"notice":"Proposes a change of the admin of the contract. The proposed new admin must call `acceptAdmin` to finalize the change. To cancel a proposal propose a different address, e.g. the zero address. Can only be called by the current admin."},"proposedAdmin()":{"notice":"Returns the proposed address to change the admin to."},"renounceAdmin()":{"notice":"Changes the admin of the contract to address zero. It's no longer possible to change the admin or upgrade the contract afterwards. Can only be called by the current admin."},"revokePauser(address)":{"notice":"Revokes the pauser role from an address. Callable only by the admin."},"setSplits((uint256,uint32)[])":{"notice":"Sets the account splits configuration. The configuration is common for all ERC-20 tokens. Nothing happens to the currently splittable funds, but when they are split after this function finishes, the new splits configuration will be used. Because anybody can call `split` on `Drips`, calling this function may be frontrun and all the currently splittable funds will be split using the old splits configuration."},"setStreams(address,(uint256,uint256)[],int128,(uint256,uint256)[],uint32,uint32,address)":{"notice":"Sets the message sender's streams configuration. Transfers funds between the message sender's wallet and the Drips contract to fulfil the change of the streams balance."},"unpause()":{"notice":"Returns to normal state. Callable only by the admin or a pauser."}},"notice":"A Drips driver implementing address-based account identification. Each address can use `AddressDriver` to control a single account ID derived from that address. ","license":"GPL-3.0-only","urls":["bzz-raw://01447946b81e304a3a2705e486f08c9a0ae9dc44b2056a0b08f108314cb9a9d6","dweb:/ipfs/QmTnMWFesRnHabFUZNSjBsPEMNqswce9g8PJevfLtEbFZn"]},"src/DriverTransferUtils.sol":{"keccak256":"0x33313dc5473d1b76bd371e5b1d1ac06c12dd1cc445576d72e8ed716638fa863c","license":"GPL-3.0-only","urls":["bzz-raw://7aa8d5327c14d1ff42afe6e6bca4f534132ddf5d429b8e32fb0b04d1a2f1077d","dweb:/ipfs/QmXapb9ZYcbH5La8SPEVJ4NVtLLgP1m2JAtdKgq6orgNME"]},"src/Managed.sol":{"keccak256":"0x0849cfa8b3466ccbab7c4aeff78adcc9c9b1fef21f40a421e1b0b1fd0509212e","license":"GPL-3.0-only","urls":["bzz-raw://4b0fb380634f519bbea832ddc00aebdfb5d6c80fd7c2e0f978e162f2e56a92cf","dweb:/ipfs/QmdPFPperaGS5EQoefhngvvQ2LytQoLgb1LdQCiBZJfCuK"]},"src/Splits.sol":{"keccak256":"0x3d7895f0a2734f6bde9df6870d4487705f5f835be699c5140d135f075292c5b3","license":"GPL-3.0-only","urls":["bzz-raw://5d4d43d70a35683e03f34fdf5c5d4bc653f86c703ce01490348d6bacca32a43c","dweb:/ipfs/QmUairngGgYVyzaL1vnni8gDoC3wKmyzaeqJuZg6FtwUjw"]},"src/Streams.sol":{"keccak256":"0x51f7bc3b140b83ba0850ef8a4a254de6236f30f493395e4f88ca5512e2b44bf0","license":"GPL-3.0-only","urls":["bzz-raw://8a227bab12101ff3c8e453b81d30289c326a02081d14916506b54165b05c2d2a","dweb:/ipfs/QmWaBFAyFfuoK7Zs9FzYs2rwKLkNjwVdPTZSbvNdBxzF7K"]}},"version":1}

returns.pausersList: The list of all the pausers, ordered arbitrarily. The list's order may change after granting or revoking the pauser role.

params.erc20: The used ERC-20 token. It must preserve amounts, so if some amount of tokens is transferred to an address, then later the same amount must be transferable from that address. Tokens which rebase the holders' balances, collect taxes on transfers, or impose any restrictions on holding or transferring tokens are not supported. If you use such tokens in the protocol, they can get stuck or lost.
params.transferTo: The address to send collected funds to

params.driverId_: The driver ID to use when calling Drips.
params.forwarder: The ERC-2771 forwarder to trust. May be the zero address.

forceCollect(address,address,address,add.details: Single entry point that enforces atomicity by calling both Drips and strategy

params.strategy: The strategy address where funds are invested.
params.transferTo: The address to send collected funds to.

params.erc20: The used ERC-20 token. It must preserve amounts, so if some amount of tokens is transferred to an address, then later the same amount must be transferable from that address. Tokens which rebase the holders' balances, collect taxes on transfers, or impose any restrictions on holding or transferring tokens are not supported. If you use such tokens in the protocol, they can get stuck or lost.

returns.isAddrPauser: True if the address is a pauser.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

params.receivers: The list of the account's splits receivers to be set. Must be sorted by the splits receivers' addresses, deduplicated and without 0 weights. Each splits receiver will be getting `weight / TOTAL_SPLITS_WEIGHT` share of the funds collected by the account. If the sum of weights of all receivers is less than `_TOTAL_SPLITS_WEIGHT`, some funds won't be split, but they will be left for the account to collect. It's valid to include the account's own `accountId` in the list of receivers, but funds split to themselves return to their splittable balance and are not collectable. This is usually unwanted, because if splitting is repeated, funds split to themselves will be again split using the current configuration. Splitting 100% to self effectively blocks splitting unless the configuration is updated.

params.balanceDelta: The streams balance change to be applied. Positive to add funds to the streams balance, negative to remove them.
params.currReceivers: The current streams receivers list. It must be exactly the same as the last list set for the sender with `setStreams`. If this is the first update, pass an empty array.
params.erc20: The used ERC-20 token. It must preserve amounts, so if some amount of tokens is transferred to an address, then later the same amount must be transferable from that address. Tokens which rebase the holders' balances, collect taxes on transfers, or impose any restrictions on holding or transferring tokens are not supported. If you use such tokens in the protocol, they can get stuck or lost.
params.maxEndHint1: An optional parameter allowing gas optimization, pass `0` to ignore it. The first hint for finding the maximum end time when all streams stop due to funds running out after the balance is updated and the new receivers list is applied. Hints have no effect on the results of calling this function, except potentially saving gas. Hints are Unix timestamps used as the starting points for binary search for the time when funds run out in the range of timestamps from the current block's to `2^32`. Hints lower than the current timestamp are ignored. You can provide zero, one or two hints. The order of hints doesn't matter. Hints are the most effective when one of them is lower than or equal to the last timestamp when funds are still streamed, and the other one is strictly larger than that timestamp,the smaller the difference between such hints, the higher gas savings. The savings are the highest possible when one of the hints is equal to the last timestamp when funds are still streamed, and the other one is larger by 1. It's worth noting that the exact timestamp of the block in which this function is executed may affect correctness of the hints, especially if they're precise. Hints don't provide any benefits when balance is not enough to cover a single second of streaming or is enough to cover all streams until timestamp `2^32`. Even inaccurate hints can be useful, and providing a single hint or two hints that don't enclose the time when funds run out can still save some gas. Providing poor hints that don't reduce the number of binary search steps may cause slightly higher gas usage than not providing any hints.
params.maxEndHint2: An optional parameter allowing gas optimization, pass `0` to ignore it. The second hint for finding the maximum end time, see `maxEndHint1` docs for more details.
params.newReceivers: The list of the streams receivers of the sender to be set. Must be sorted by the receivers' addresses, deduplicated and without 0 amtPerSecs.
params.transferTo: The address to send funds to in case of decreasing balance

returns.realBalanceDelta: The actually applied streams balance change.

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

acceptAdmin().notice: Applies a proposed change of the admin of the contract. Sets the proposed admin to the zero address. Can only be called by the proposed admin.

admin().notice: Returns the address of the current admin.

allPausers().notice: Returns all the addresses with the pauser role.

calcAccountId(address).notice: Calculates the account ID for an address. Every account ID is a 256-bit integer constructed by concatenating: `driverId (32 bits) | zeros (64 bits) | addr (160 bits)`.

collect(address,address).notice: Collects the account's received already split funds and transfers them out of the Drips contract.

drips().notice: The Drips address used by this driver.

driverId().notice: The driver ID which this driver uses when calling Drips.

emitAccountMetadata((bytes32,bytes)[]).notice: Emits the account metadata for the message sender. The keys and the values are not standardized by the protocol, it's up to the users to establish and follow conventions to ensure compatibility with the consumers.

forceCollect(address,address,address,add.notice: Force collect - for when funds are in YieldManager instead of Drips vault

give(uint256,address,uint128).notice: Gives funds from the message sender to the receiver. The receiver can split and collect them immediately. Transfers the funds to be given from the message sender's wallet to the Drips contract.

grantPauser(address).notice: Grants the pauser role to an address. Callable only by the admin.

isPaused().notice: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

isPauser(address).notice: Checks if an address is a pauser.

pause().notice: Triggers stopped state. Callable only by the admin or a pauser.

proposeNewAdmin(address).notice: Proposes a change of the admin of the contract. The proposed new admin must call `acceptAdmin` to finalize the change. To cancel a proposal propose a different address, e.g. the zero address. Can only be called by the current admin.

proposedAdmin().notice: Returns the proposed address to change the admin to.

renounceAdmin().notice: Changes the admin of the contract to address zero. It's no longer possible to change the admin or upgrade the contract afterwards. Can only be called by the current admin.

revokePauser(address).notice: Revokes the pauser role from an address. Callable only by the admin.

setSplits((uint256,uint32)[]).notice: Sets the account splits configuration. The configuration is common for all ERC-20 tokens. Nothing happens to the currently splittable funds, but when they are split after this function finishes, the new splits configuration will be used. Because anybody can call `split` on `Drips`, calling this function may be frontrun and all the currently splittable funds will be split using the old splits configuration.

setStreams(address,(uint256,uint256)[],i.notice: Sets the message sender's streams configuration. Transfers funds between the message sender's wallet and the Drips contract to fulfil the change of the streams balance.

unpause().notice: Returns to normal state. Callable only by the admin or a pauser.
